original_hypothesis.title: Missing GenesisManager Initialization Bricks Epoch Upkeep
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The EpochController contract does not set the genesisManager address during initialization. When performUpkeep is called, it attempts to execute genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip(). Because genesisManager is address(0), this call reverts, completely blocking the automated epoch transition, emission distribution, and fee claiming processes.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a critical configuration gap. If the protocol relies on automation to flip epochs, the missing initialization will cause the automation to fail continuously until an admin manually intervenes.
deployment_assumptions: genesisManager is not set via setGenesisManager before the first epoch flip
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: V1/upgradeable/deployKeeper.js does not call setGenesisManager after deployment.

proof_of_signal.pattern: genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip(); // reverts if genesisManager is address(0)

preconditions: setGenesisManager has not been called by the owner
Epoch condition is met and performUpkeep is triggered

initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but causes severe operational failure

economic_questions: Is there a fallback mechanism to distribute rewards if performUpkeep fails?
validation_requirements: Check deployment scripts for setGenesisManager calls
Verify if distributeAll can be called directly by anyone (it can, but performUpkeep is the intended automated path)

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Delays emissions and fee distributions, causing user panic

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm revert on zero address call

validation_result.mechanism: Missing null-check on uninitialized interface call
validation_result.impact_summary: EpochController.performUpkeep() reverts when genesisManager is not set, blocking automated epoch transitions. Manual workarounds (calling distributeAll/distributeFees directly) exist but the primary automation path is broken.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The EpochController.initialize() function at EpochController.sol:25-30 accepts three parameters (_minter, _permissionsRegistry, _gaugeManager) but does not set genesisManager. The state variable `IGenesisPoolManager public genesisManager` defaults to address(0). In performUpkeep() at line 47, `genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip()` is called unconditionally. In Solidity 0.8.13, calling a function on an interface variable pointing to address(0) triggers an extcodesize check that fails (no code at address(0)), causing a revert. This blocks the automated epoch transition before distributeAll() and distributeFees() are ever reached. The defect is in the code itself: performUpkeep lacks a null-check for genesisManager before calling it.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: EpochController.sol lines 25-30 show initialize() does not set genesisManager. Lines 41-50 show performUpkeep() unconditionally calls genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip() before distributeAll() and distributeFees(). The setGenesisManager function exists at lines 72-75 but must be called separately by the owner. No deployment script calls setGenesisManager on EpochController.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: genesisManager is not set in initialize(). It defaults to address(0) (the zero-value of IGenesisPoolManager type).

genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip() is called unconditionally without checking if genesisManager is set. When genesisManager is address(0), Solidity 0.8.13 reverts due to extcodesize check on the zero address.

calling_sequence: 1. Chainlink automation or EPOCH_MANAGER calls performUpkeep()
2. Access control check passes (msg.sender == automationRegistry or has EPOCH_MANAGER role)
3. checkUpkeep('0x') calls IMinter(minter).check() which returns true (epoch condition met)
7. distributeAll() and distributeFees() are never reached
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path is EpochController.performUpkeep -> genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip(). Since genesisManager is IGenesisPoolManager type initialized to address(0), and Solidity 0.8.13 performs extcodesize checks on typed interface calls, calling any function on address(0) reverts. The call at line 47 blocks execution before lines 48-49 (distributeAll and distributeFees).

deployment_assumptions_checked: genesisManager is not set via setGenesisManager before the first epoch flip
config_validation.deployment_evidence: V1/upgradeable/deployKeeper.js deploys EpochController using old constructor pattern (not initialize). deployment-flows/scripts/contract-deployments/set-chainlink.js sets automationRegistry and automationRegistry2 but does NOT call setGenesisManager. No deployment script in the provided codebase calls setGenesisManager on EpochController.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack per se, but a configuration/code defect. After deployment and initialization of EpochController, if the owner does not call setGenesisManager() before the first epoch boundary, performUpkeep() will revert on every invocation. Chainlink automation will repeatedly fail, and EPOCH_MANAGER role holders cannot flip the epoch through EpochController. However, GaugeManager.distributeAll() and GaugeManager.distributeFees() are externally callable by anyone (no access control), so a manual workaround exists. MinterUpgradeable.update_period() is also permissionless. The automated path is broken but the protocol is not permanently bricked.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity for automated operations. The primary automated epoch transition mechanism via Chainlink automation is completely blocked until an admin intervenes by calling setGenesisManager(). During the downtime: (1) automated epoch transitions fail, (2) emission distributions are delayed, (3) fee distributions are delayed. Manual workarounds exist (calling distributeAll/distributeFees/update_period directly) but require off-chain monitoring and intervention. The performPreUpkeep function is similarly affected (calls genesisManager.check() and genesisManager.checkBeforeEpochFlip()).
recommended_mitigations: Add genesisManager parameter to initialize() function: function initialize(address _minter, address _permissionsRegistry, address _gaugeManager, address _genesisManager)
Add null-check before calling genesisManager in performUpkeep: if(address(genesisManager) != address(0)) genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip();
Add deployment script step that calls setGenesisManager() immediately after initialization
Apply same fix to performPreUpkeep and checkUpPrekeep which also call genesisManager without null-check
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy EpochController behind proxy. 2. Call initialize(_minter, _permissionsRegistry, _gaugeManager) without setting genesisManager. 3. Set automationRegistry. 4. Wait for epoch condition to be met (IMinter.check() returns true). 5. Call performUpkeep('0x') from automationRegistry. 6. Observe revert at genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip() call.
uncertainties: The IGenesisPoolManager interface is not provided in scope - we assume checkAtEpochFlip() is a standard external function that would trigger the extcodesize revert when called on address(0)
There may be deployment scripts not provided that call setGenesisManager, though none were found in the available scripts
additional_testing_needed: Verify that calling a function on IGenesisPoolManager(address(0)) indeed reverts in Solidity 0.8.13 compiled with optimizer runs=10
Check if there are additional deployment/configuration scripts not provided that set genesisManager
Test the manual workaround path (calling distributeAll/distributeFees directly) to confirm it works as a backup
